search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae flank pain dysuria telehealth guideline UAE PDF
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng118 flank pain renal stone imaging
site:gov.ae renal colic flank pain guideline UAE
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Treatment protocols were outside documentation scope.
Automatic diagnosis or escalation conclusions were prohibited.
Non-official summaries were not accepted as exact evidence.
population_applicability: Partial: the DHA source concerns dysuria and complicated urinary infection, and NICE NG118 concerns suspected or confirmed stones; the broad flank-pain workflow encodes neither cause nor all age and pregnancy qualifiers.
setting_applicability: Partial: DHA is telehealth guidance and NICE imaging is recommendation-qualified; physical examination, diagnosis, treatment, and emergency decisions remain outside the mapping.
UAE_applicability: DHA guidance is direct for Dubai telehealth urinary-history and red-flag documentation. NICE imaging requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 PDF and current NICE NG118 recommendations were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer DHA dysuria issue was identified; NICE NG118 remains current after its May 2026 minor update.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped flank-pain, duration, urinary, fever, nausea or vomiting, urine-output, and trauma-history fields without inferring cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped fever with flank pain or tenderness, visible haematuria, vomiting, and CVA-tenderness prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped urinalysis and qualified renal-function documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-reviewed imaging only in a suspected or confirmed stone context; no stone diagnosis is inferred.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed CT or ultrasound imaging with age and pregnancy qualifiers.

unresolved_source_gaps: Character, radiation, previous-stone history, solitary-kidney status, sepsis assessment, general vitals, blood pressure, temperature, CBC, CRP, previous records, generic plan, referral, safety-netting, fixed intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer stone disease, pyelonephritis, significant obstruction, infection, or haemodynamic instability.
Diagnosis, investigation selection, treatment, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
